commit.message:
feat: sim/live data flow switching (#2)

* feat: add sim/live data flow switching

Adds the ability to toggle between simulation and live (Kafka) data
sources in real-time, without reconnecting the WebSocket.

Backend:
- Flow-prefixed channel groups (realtime_{sim|live}_{channel}_org_{id})
  so sim and live broadcasts are isolated
- WebSocket set_flow action to switch flows, re-joining all groups
- available_flows reported on auth (based on KAFKA_AVAILABLE flag)
- Simulation snapshot cache: records detections near active incidents
  (±50 channels, 60s window) for IncidentSnapshotView
- IncidentSnapshotView checks simulation cache before ClickHouse
- IncidentListView returns empty instead of 503 when CH unavailable

Frontend:
- RealtimeProvider exposes flow, availableFlows, setFlow, onFlowChange
- FlowToggle component with disabled state for unavailable sources
- Channel helper dots on map for section creation aid
- Prototype page and SidePanel wired to flow context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: dev-frontend build, flow toggle style, and REALTIME_SOURCE docs

- Makefile: switch dev-frontend from `npm run dev` to `build + preview`
  to avoid Vite dev server serving stale cached assets. Unset VITE_*
  env vars before build so the frontend .env is used (not shell env).
- FlowToggle: use proto-surface/proto-surface-raised instead of
  proto-base/proto-accent so the toggle blends with the side panel.
- .env.example: document all four REALTIME_SOURCE values (kafka, sim,
  both, auto) with clear descriptions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: simulation incident min duration + re-enable frontend typecheck in CI

- Enforce 2-minute minimum real-time duration for simulated incidents.
  Previously, the shortest incidents (slowdown) lasted only ~20 seconds
  after the 15x time compression, appearing to resolve instantly.
- Re-enable frontend typecheck step in CI — `tsc --noEmit` now passes
  cleanly (src/lib/ modules are committed and type mismatches resolved).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: mark completed TODO items (typecheck CI, incident duration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: incident snapshots with full ±60s window and live polling

Backend:
- Rolling detection buffer per fiber keeps last 60s of detections
- When an incident spawns, snapshot is seeded from the buffer (pre-incident data)
- Fixed window: detected_at ± 60s, marked complete when window elapses
- API response includes `complete` flag so frontend knows when to stop polling

Frontend:
- Group snapshot detections by second (was by minute → single data point)
- Poll snapshot API every 1s until complete, updating the chart live
- Show "collecting..." indicator while snapshot window is still open

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: incident resolution timezone bug and snapshot sync frequency

- Incident resolution used time.mktime (local time) to parse a UTC
  timestamp, causing incidents to appear already expired in non-UTC
  timezones. Added detected_at_ms field to Incident to avoid
  UTC/local parsing entirely.
- Snapshot cache syncs every 1s (20 ticks) instead of every 5s (100
  ticks) so frontend 1s polling gets fresh data including post-incident
  detections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add incident replay player plan and update TODO

New plan in TODO/plans/incident-player.md: interactive playback of
incident snapshots with time slider, map detection dots, and chart
cursor sync. Also marks snapshot fix as complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: incident marker on snapshot charts + updated replay plan

- Add dashed red vertical ReferenceLine at the incident time on all
  three snapshot chart tabs (speed, flow, occupancy)
- incidentTime prop threaded through TimeSeriesChart → TimeSeriesChartInner
- Updated incident-player plan: marker already done, mini map is a
  separate embedded Mapbox instance with dots/3D toggle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: full 2-min snapshot chart, zero lint warnings, zero mypy errors

- Snapshot chart now pre-fills a fixed 2-minute X-axis (incident ± 60s)
  so the incident marker stays centered from the start; empty seconds
  fill in progressively as data arrives
- Fix all 14 ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings across 5 files
- Fix all 40 backend mypy type errors across 6 files (TypedDicts for
  simulation configs, list invariance fixes in road_snap, typed spawn
  points, explicit FiberConfig construction)
- Add simulation overhaul plan (TODO/plans/simulation-overhaul.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: compute occupancy in snapshots, spawn incidents near vehicles

- Snapshot chart now computes occupancy from speed and flow using the
  standard traffic formula (same as live section stats)
- Incidents spawn near existing vehicles instead of random channels,
  ensuring snapshots always have detection data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add expandable side panel TODO item

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add channel-to-road mapping TODO item

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: widen snapshot channel radius and prevent Recharts dimension warnings

Increase SNAPSHOT_CHANNEL_RADIUS from 50 to 100 (~1km) so the rolling
buffer captures pre-incident detections from vehicles approaching at
speed. Add minWidth/minHeight={0} to ResponsiveContainer to suppress
console warnings during CSS panel transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable chart re-animation on snapshot poll to prevent line drift

Recharts re-animated the entire line on every 1s poll update, causing
the visual effect of left-side data disappearing while right-side data
appeared. Disable animation and add connectNulls for stable rendering.
Also improve snapshot loading placeholder styling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: aggregate snapshot data in backend, drop payload from 1.2MB to ~3KB

Backend now stores 1-second aggregation buckets (avg speed, flow, occupancy)
instead of raw detections. The snapshot API returns 120 pre-computed points.

- Fixes sliding line bug: old SNAPSHOT_MAX_DETECTIONS cap was trimming
  pre-incident data as new detections arrived
- Fixes partial-second dip: current (incomplete) bucket is skipped until
  its second has fully elapsed
- ClickHouse path also aggregates server-side via GROUP BY
- Frontend no longer does any aggregation math — just renders points
- Payload per poll: ~1.2 MB → ~3 KB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add PR review agent system prompt

Tailored to SequoIA conventions — covers hard rules, architectural
invariants, code quality checks, and performance concerns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: track frontend src/lib/ files ignored by overly broad gitignore

The root .gitignore had `lib/` (Python convention for compiled C
extensions) which matched all lib/ directories including the frontend's
src/lib/. Changed to `/lib/` (root-anchored) so only the top-level
lib/ is ignored. Adds 15 previously untracked frontend source files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review — org-scope, thread safety, i18n, dead code, 503

- Raise NotFound instead of falling through to ClickHouse when sim
  incident exists but org doesn't own its fiber (security fix)
- Replace thread-unsafe settings.KAFKA_AVAILABLE with cache.set/get
  backed by Redis, safe for multi-process deployments
- Move FlowToggle hardcoded strings to en.json/fr.json
- Remove unused availableFlowsRef in RealtimeProvider
- Return 503 instead of empty list when ClickHouse is down in
  IncidentListView

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: flow-aware snapshot queries and cache timeout

- Skip simulation cache for snapshot requests when flow=live, so live
  flow users only get ClickHouse data
- Frontend passes current flow as query param to snapshot endpoint
- Add timeout=None to cache.set("kafka_available") to prevent silent
  expiry after Django's default 300s TTL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent Recharts negative dimension warnings

Set ResponsiveContainer minWidth/minHeight to 1 instead of 0 so
Recharts never receives negative dimensions when the chart container
is not yet fully laid out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: refresh token proactively to avoid 401 console warnings

The auth heartbeat was calling verifyToken() every 12 min, which sent the
(possibly expired) access token and triggered a browser-logged 401 before
the JS retry logic could refresh it. Now calls attemptTokenRefresh()
directly using the httpOnly cookie, eliminating the unnecessary 401 cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: track flow switch rollback, type hints, and occupancy duplication in TODO

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: expand SHM TODO with pipeline, simulation, and frontend details

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: separate sim/live data paths, deduplicate SHM broadcasts, update conventions

- IncidentListView: skip sim fallback when ?flow=live, matching the
  snapshot endpoint behavior (prevents sim data leaking into live flow)
- Frontend: thread flow param through fetchIncidents and useIncidents
- SHM broadcasts: extract _broadcast_shm helper in both simulation.py
  and kafka_bridge.py, replacing inline group_send duplication
- CLAUDE.md: add issue-driven workflow and PR hygiene guidelines
- PR template: add consistency checklist (flow-aware endpoints, shared
  helpers, i18n)
- TODO: track broadcast unification and infra_org_map elimination

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update TODO.md with issue references and add #29, #30

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: org-scope sim incident fallback, bump retention to 1 month

- IncidentListView: filter sim fallback incidents by user's org fibers
  (previously returned unfiltered sim cache to all orgs)
- Bump resolved incident retention from 50 to 10,000 (~1 month at
  ~360 incidents/day, ~55 MB)
- TODO: add incident browsing/navigation task

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: extract FlowAwareMixin for sim/live data source routing

Replace inline ?flow= query param checks with a shared mixin that
encapsulates flow parsing, sim-allowed gating, and org-scoped sim
fallback logic. Views inherit FlowAwareMixin and call self._allow_sim()
or self.get_sim_fallback() instead of repeating the pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: centralize org-scoped fiber filtering in fibers/utils

Add filter_by_org() for list filtering and fiber_belongs_to_org() for
single-item checks. Replace all inline strip_directional_suffix + set
membership checks across views.py, consumers.py, and the FlowAwareMixin
with calls to these shared utilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move FlowAwareMixin from shared to monitoring, add #31 to TODO

FlowAwareMixin depends on simulation_manager and fibers, making it
not a true shared utility. Move to apps/monitoring/mixins.py where
the views that use it live.

Also add issue reference [#31] for incident browsing TODO item.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: only fall back to sim incidents when ClickHouse is unavailable

An empty result from ClickHouse is valid ("no incidents") and should
not trigger the simulation fallback. Previously `if not incidents:`
matched both `None` (unavailable) and `[]` (empty), causing sim data
to leak into the response when ClickHouse was up but had no incidents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: strict sim/live flow isolation, deduplicate shared utils

Flow isolation — data never crosses between flows:
- FlowAwareMixin: replace fallback model (_allow_sim/get_sim_fallback)
  with strict routing (_is_sim/_get_sim_data). Sim flow never touches
  ClickHouse, live flow never touches simulation cache.
- IncidentListView: branch at top into _get_sim_incidents (sim cache
  only) vs _get_live_incidents (ClickHouse only, 503 if down).
- IncidentSnapshotView: same pattern — _get_sim_snapshot vs
  _get_live_snapshot with no cross-flow fallback.
- Cache keys now include flow to prevent cross-flow cache hits.

Backend deduplication:
- Extract build_org_cache_key, paginate_queryset, org_filter_queryset
  into apps/shared/utils.py (was duplicated in admin_api, fibers,
  monitoring, reporting views).

Frontend:
- Add useFlowReset hook — DRYs the identical useEffect/onFlowChange
  pattern across useDetections, useLiveStats, useIncidents,
  useVehicleSim, useWaterfallBuffer.

Performance:
- simulation._record_incident_detections: iterate snapshot values
  instead of all 10k+ incidents.

Docs:
- TODO: add #33 (move sections from ClickHouse to PostgreSQL).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update TODO #16 — drop deleted cache key functions from untyped list

* fix: bounded KAFKA_AVAILABLE TTL, cache org in consumer, remove dead code

- kafka_bridge: replace timeout=None with 60s TTL, refreshed every 30s
  so a crashed bridge is detected within a minute
- consumers: cache Organization on self._org during _setup_user instead
  of hitting DB on every _query_initial_incidents/_query_initial_fibers
- Remove unused useIncidentSnapshot hook (SidePanel calls the API
  directly with flow param)
- CLAUDE.md: reference tools/prompts/pr-review-agent.md for PR reviews
- TODO: add #34 (Kafka retention policy cleanup)

* fix: i18n "Data Source" label, explicit flow params on all broadcasts

- Move hardcoded "Data Source" string to i18n (flow.label) in en/fr
- Pass flow="sim" explicitly on all 3 simulation broadcasts (detections,
  incidents, counts) instead of relying on default parameter
- Pass flow="live" explicitly on all 3 kafka_bridge broadcasts (replay
  detections, new incidents, resolved incidents)
- Create issue #35 for IncidentActionView flow-awareness (separate PR)

* chore: disable simulation detection noise, add Kafka retention TODO

- Zero out channel and speed noise in _generate_detections (commented
  out original values for easy re-enable)
- Add TODO #34 for removing competing Kafka retention policies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add flow isolation, broadcast scoping, async safety, and cache rules to PR review prompt

Five new architectural invariants:
- Strict sim/live flow isolation (no cross-flow fallback, explicit flow params)
- Org-scoped broadcasts (use shared helpers, not inline group_send)
- No blocking in async code paths (sync_to_async, asyncio.Lock)
- Bounded in-memory caches (size cap + TTL eviction)
- Org-scoped cache keys (include org_id and flow)

* docs: add WebSocket reconnecting banner rework to TODO [#37]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add task tracking consolidation and section overlay fix to TODO [#38, #39]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: deduplicate broadcast_shm, flow-aware stats, remove async locks, persist flow

- Extract shared broadcast_shm into apps/realtime/broadcast.py [#17]
- Make StatsView flow-aware via FlowAwareMixin with sim stats cache [#40]
- Remove threading.Lock from simulation caches (atomic ref swap)
- Persist user's flow choice across WebSocket reconnects [#15]
- Reinstate useIncidentSnapshot hook with polling + stale-request safety
- Make _org_group_name flow param required (remove dead default)
- Add type hints to shared/utils.py (paginate_queryset, org_filter_queryset) [#16]
- Tighten KAFKA_AVAILABLE_TTL from 60s/30s to 10s/5s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix ledger state not loading and day navigation issues

The profitability ledger failed to load saved state on every game restart
because Plugin.Services was null during the ProfitabilityLedger constructor.
ResolveProductName accessed Plugin.Services.ProductCatalog without a
null-conditional check on Services, causing a NullReferenceException that
silently discarded all historical data. Each session started fresh with
1 day, making day navigation impossible.

- Add null-conditional operator to all Plugin.Services.ProductCatalog checks
- Fix MoveSelection early-return bug that skipped applying the delta on
  first navigation click
- Show day range (Day X/Y) in primary metrics header
- Add diagnostic logging to MoveSelection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Modernize project from Java 1.5/Spring 3.1 to Java 17/Spring 6.2.3

Upgrade platform: Java 17, Spring 6.2.3, Spring Batch 5.2.2, JUnit 5,
HSQLDB 2.7.4, SLF4J/Logback replacing commons-logging/log4j.

Java modernization: Ledger as immutable record, java.time replacing
java.util.Date, BigDecimal for monetary fields, var, parameterized
logging, extracted helper methods, cleaned up signatures.

Spring Batch improvements: eliminated custom DAO layer (3 files) in
favor of JdbcBatchItemWriter, added LedgerProcessor for validation
and normalization, added skip/retry fault tolerance, migrated tests
to @SpringBatchTest, extracted shared base-batch-context.xml.

Observability: MDC context propagation, Micrometer metrics with
BatchMetricsLogger, enhanced job/step listeners with duration and
commit/rollback counts, structured logback pattern, rolling file
appender, full listener coverage on all three jobs.

Testing: expanded from 1 trivial test to 14 tests covering unit
tests for Ledger, LedgerMapper, LedgerRowMapper and integration
tests for all three batch jobs with proper assertions.

Added .gitignore and removed Eclipse IDE files from tracking.

#42 Add extensible broadcastState hooks; sync vine phase/anchor/decay to proxies

PlantSeed.broadcastState() now calls GetExtraBroadcastStateSize() /
OnWriteBroadcastState() / OnReadBroadcastState() so subclasses can
append typed fields to the stateSync payload without touching the base
class serialisation.

ViningPlantSeed overrides all three to include _growthPhase, _vineAnchored,
_isDecaying, and the anchored world pos/rot. OnReadBroadcastState re-applies
scale after UpdateVisuals() resets it, so proxies see the correct vine/fruit
scale the moment the stateSync arrives.
